subject: Physics
title: Bohr's Atomic Model
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Bohr's Atomic Model.

hook.text: In 1913, Niels Bohr proposed that electrons orbit atoms only in specific allowed energy levels.

buildup.text: Classical physics said orbiting electrons should radiate energy and spiral into the nucleus instantly.

discovery.text: Bohr said electrons can only exist in fixed orbits. They jump between levels by absorbing or emitting photons.

twist.text: His model explained hydrogen perfectly but failed for larger atoms. It was a brilliant stepping stone.

climax.text: Bohr's quantized orbits paved the way for full quantum mechanics a decade later.

punchline.text: Electrons don't spiral. They jump. Quantum leaps are real.

quiz.question: What did Bohr's model say about electron orbits?
options: Electrons exist only in specific allowed energy levels
Electrons orbit randomly at any distance
Electrons are stationary inside the nucleus
